source.title: V-Log/V-Gamut REFERENCE MANUAL
source.url_note: The older URL cited in early research (pro-av.panasonic.net/en/varicam/common/pdf/...) now serves an HTML page; this cinema_camera_varicam_eva path serves the PDF as of 2026-07-19.

notes: Encode: out = 5.6*in + 0.125 for in < cut1; out = c*log10(in + b) + d for in >= cut1; cut1 = 0.01, b = 0.00873, c = 0.241514, d = 0.598206 (in = reflection, out = normalized signal). Decode linear branch: out = (in - 0.125) / 5.6. 10-bit code values quoted from the manual: grey 18% reflection = 433, black 0% = 128, white 90% = 602. All three cross-checked against colour-science 0.4.7 log_decoding_VLog on 2026-07-19: deltas 2e-5, 8e-5, 2.6e-3 (white delta is table CV rounding).

vectors.name: black0
vectors.quote: black output code with reflection of 0% as 128

vectors.name: grey18
vectors.quote: Gray output code with reflection of 18% is defined as 433

vectors.name: white90
vectors.quote: white output code with reflection of 90% as 602
